title: 2024 Singapore gender pay gap (unadjusted median comparison)
summary: Unadjusted median total annual compensation for Singapore respondents paid in SGD, 2024 survey cycle (n=171: 58 men, 113 women), calculated with the same method as the 2023 report's 42% figure: man median S$92,400 versus woman median S$76,000, a gap of 17.7% with women earning less. At every seniority level with a publishable cohort (Junior IC, Mid-level IC, and Senior IC), women's median compensation is at or above men's; Lead-and-above and other levels fall below the minimum safe cohort of 10 and are suppressed. The aggregate gap tracks the seniority mix rather than within-level pay: women are concentrated in the lower-paying Junior IC and Mid-level IC tiers (60.2% of women versus 44.8% of men), while men are more represented from Lead-and-above upward (27.6% of men versus 14.2% of women).
methodology: Raw survey responses filtered to Singapore respondents paid in SGD; median total annual compensation per gender cohort and per seniority level; unadjusted, not matched by role; MIN_SAFE_COHORT = 10 enforced before publication.
